dev.jeka.core.api.project

Class JkProjectTesting

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.project.JkProjectTesting

  • public class JkProjectTesting
extends java.lang.Object
    Handles project testing step. This involves both test compilation and run. Users can configure inner phases by chaining runnables. They also can modify JkJavaCompilerToolChain, JkJavaCompileSpec for test compilation and JkTestProcessor, JkTestSelection for test run.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      JkProjectCompilation compilation 
      JkRunnables postActions 
      JkTestProcessor testProcessor
      The processor running the tests.
      JkTestSelection testSelection
      Tests to be run.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      JkProjectTesting apply(java.util.function.Consumer<JkProjectTesting> consumer)
      Applies the given consumer to the current instance of JkProjectTesting.
      JkTestProcessor createDefaultTestProcessor()
      Creates a default test processor for running tests.
      JkTestSelection createDefaultTestSelection()
      Creates a default test selection for the current project.
      java.nio.file.Path getReportDir()
      Returns the directory path where the test reports are stored.
      JkPathSequence getTestClasspath()
      Returns the classpath to run the test.
      boolean isBreakOnFailures()
      Returns if #run should fail (throwing a IllegalArgumentException) if test result has failures.
      boolean isSkipped()
      Returns if the tests should be skipped.
      void run()
      Performs entire test phase, including : compile regular code if needed perform pre test tasks if present compile test code and process test resources execute compiled tests execute post tesks if present
      void runIfNeeded()
      As #run, but performs only if not already done.
      JkProjectTesting setBreakOnFailures(boolean breakOnFailures)
      Sets whether the execution of tests should break (throw an IllegalArgumentException) if there are test failures.
      JkProjectTesting setReportDir(java.lang.String reportDir)
      Sets the directory path where the test reports are stored.
      JkProjectTesting setSkipped(boolean skipped)
      Specifies if the tests should be skipped.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • compilation

        public final JkProjectCompilation compilation

      • testProcessor

        public final JkTestProcessor testProcessor
        The processor running the tests.

      • testSelection

        public final JkTestSelection testSelection
        Tests to be run.

      • postActions

        public final JkRunnables postActions

    • Method Detail

      • apply

        public JkProjectTesting apply(java.util.function.Consumer<JkProjectTesting> consumer)
        Applies the given consumer to the current instance of JkProjectTesting. This method allows you to modify the JkProjectTesting object using the provided consumer.

      • getTestClasspath

        public JkPathSequence getTestClasspath()
        Returns the classpath to run the test. It consists in test classes + prod classes + dependencies defined in testing/compile.

      • isSkipped

        public boolean isSkipped()
        Returns if the tests should be skipped.

      • setSkipped

        public JkProjectTesting setSkipped(boolean skipped)
        Specifies if the tests should be skipped.

      • isBreakOnFailures

        public boolean isBreakOnFailures()
        Returns if #run should fail (throwing a IllegalArgumentException) if test result has failures.

      • setBreakOnFailures

        public JkProjectTesting setBreakOnFailures(boolean breakOnFailures)
        Sets whether the execution of tests should break (throw an IllegalArgumentException) if there are test failures.

      • getReportDir

        public java.nio.file.Path getReportDir()
        Returns the directory path where the test reports are stored.

      • setReportDir

        public JkProjectTesting setReportDir(java.lang.String reportDir)
        Sets the directory path where the test reports are stored.

      • run

        public void run()
        Performs entire test phase, including :
        • compile regular code if needed
        • perform pre test tasks if present
        • compile test code and process test resources
        • execute compiled tests
        • execute post tesks if present

      • runIfNeeded

        public void runIfNeeded()
        As #run, but performs only if not already done.

      • createDefaultTestSelection

        public JkTestSelection createDefaultTestSelection()
        Creates a default test selection for the current project. The default test selection includes the compiled test classes

      • createDefaultTestProcessor

        public JkTestProcessor createDefaultTestProcessor()
        Creates a default test processor for running tests.
        Returns:
        a default JkTestProcessor object.
